revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:朝子塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.260805555556|N|139.36127777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Chōshizuka Kofun''' (朝子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|qid=Q386179|lt=Ōta, Gunma|1=Ōta, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It has been designated as a prefectural historic site. == Overview == The kofun is situated on a low plateau at an elevation of 34 meters. The front rectangular portion has a trapezoidal plan and is relatively flat. At the top of the rear circular portion, a square platform measuring about 10 meters on each side has been constructed. The surrounding moat is roughly the same shape as the mound, with the overall area, including the outer moat, measuring 180 meters in length and 120 meters in width. The outer surface of the two-tiered mound is covered with small stones sourced from the {{ill|Watarase River|en|Watarase River|qid=Q3566607|lt=Watarase River|1=Watarase River}} system. The site has been excavated three times, in 1956, 1991, and 1999, by {{ill|Gunma University|en|Gunma University|qid=Q178017|lt=Gunma University|1=Gunma University}} and the Ōta City Board of Education. Artifacts unearthed include ordinary cylindrical haniwa, special stand-shaped cylindrical haniwa, morning glory-shaped haniwa, as well as figurative haniwa representing jars, houses, shields, and sunshades (kinugasa), along with jars with perforated bases. Cylindrical haniwa were arranged around the base of the mound and its terraces, as well as on the square platform at the top of the rear circular portion, where they formed two rectangular enclosures. At the four corners of these enclosures, jar-shaped haniwa were placed atop the cylindrical ones. Shield-shaped and sunshade-shaped haniwa, as well as perforated jars, were erected outside the square platform. Although vertical burial chambers are thought to exist within the rectangular enclosures on the platform, they remain unexcavated, and their details are unknown. The Chōshizuka Kofun is believed to date from the late 4th to early 5th century. There are indications of a connection with the nearby {{ill|Ishidagawa Site|ja|石田川遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishidagawa Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石田川 (太田市)|1=Ishida River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q70750988}}, located about 1.5 kilometers to the west.<ref>[http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0060-005chiiki-sawano/kofun.html Chōshizuka Kofun] - Ōta City</ref> Additionally, it has been suggested that this kofun may be related to the {{ill|Tamonoko Kofun|lt=Tamonoko Kofun|lt=Tanomoshi Kofun|ja|頼母子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomoshi Kofun|qq=}}, which dates from the mid-to-late 4th century and was formerly located to the west of Chōshizuka. == Dimensions == * Mound length: 123.5 meters<ref>{{Cite book|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|editor=Ōtsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburō|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|year=2002|pages=298-299|qq=}}</ref> * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 62 meters ** Height: 11.8 meters * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 48 meters ** Height: 6.8 meters == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|en|Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|qid=Q11444167|lt=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun|1=Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun}} - The largest kofun in eastern Japan, located in Ōta City. * {{ill|Tōgoku Bunka Rekishi Kaidō|ja|東国文化歴史街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgoku Bunka Rekishi Kaidō|1=Tōgoku Bunka Rekishi Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11526386}} - Partially cuts into the rear circular portion of this kofun and includes many archaeological sites along its route. * {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|en|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|qid=Q11577925|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|1=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun}} - Similar special stand-shaped cylindrical haniwa have been found at this kofun. == External Links == * [http://www.city.ota.gunma.jp/005gyosei/0170-009kyoiku-bunka/kankoubutu/files/isidagawa.pdf Ishidagawa Site Pamphlet] - Ōta City
{{translated page|ja|朝子塚古墳|version=76363566|comment=Imported full ja history}}
